title: Writing an email
explanation:
Writing an email

When you write a short email in English:

  • Start with Hi [name], or Dear [name],
  • Use simple sentences about you
  • End with Thanks, or Best wishes, + your name

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the best phrase to complete each sentence in an email.

questions.feedback: Hi Tom, is a friendly opening.

questions.text: Asking about someone: [*How are you?|How old are you?|Where are you?]
Giving news: [*I'm on holiday.|I'm on the holiday.|I'm in holiday.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct phrase for each gap.

questions.text: I hope you are ____. | *well | good | fine well
A formal opening for a teacher: [*Dear Mr Smith,|Hi Mr Smith hey,|Yo Smith,]
To say you reply later: [*I'll write again soon.|I write again soon.|Writing again soon.]
Correct sign-off: [*Thanks, Anna|Thanks Anna to|Anna thanks]
Inviting a friend: [*Do you want to come?|You want come?|Come you want?]
